title: festuca ovina subsp. makutrensis
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. makutrensis, also known as a sub-species of sheep's fescue, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of East Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Festuca ovina subsp. makutrensis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
